maestro.description: SDLC orchestrator. Starts and manages the full development lifecycle loop: requirements → architecture → implementation → testing → code review. Use this to develop a feature end-to-end.

requirement-engineer.description: Gathers and documents software requirements. Produces PRD, user stories with acceptance criteria, and constraints. Start here for new features.

architect.description: Designs system architecture, API contracts, database schema, and writes ADRs. Run after requirements are complete.

frontend-dev.description: Implements frontend UI features with React/TypeScript. Handles components, state, API integration, accessibility, and frontend tests.

backend-dev.description: Implements backend APIs, business logic, database access, authentication, and backend tests.

tester.description: Validates acceptance criteria, writes and runs test plans, produces bug reports. Run after implementation.

code-reviewer.description: Reviews code for correctness, security, performance, and quality. Approves or requests changes. Final gate before feature is complete.
